mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising retail sales indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion. This joint signal increases forward earnings estimates and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements that directly translate into higher corporate margins and sustained equity outperformance over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, producing negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of high-INDPRO growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand, which immediately lifts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above trend consistently coincides with negative or flat 5-day forward returns, yielding a negative rank correlation over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5 percent, tighter financial conditions compress equity valuations and increase borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 3.5 percent, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with a stable unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor market expansion rather than cyclical noise. This joint confirmation reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 50 percent of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, accelerating retail sales combined with rising housing permits signal peak consumer spending. This momentum drives short-term equity outperformance but reverses once the cycle turns, yielding positive 5-day returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of late-cycle instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
